sourceMovie.title: Pandorum
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Pandorum works as a action movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The action and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Extinction
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pandorum clicked for you, Extinction (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Oblivion
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pandorum clicked for you, Oblivion (2013) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and mystery, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Pandorum clicked for you, Twelve Monkeys (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in mystery and science fiction, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Pandorum?
faq.answer: Try Extinction, Oblivion, Twelve Monkeys—each shares DNA with Pandorum while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Pandorum?
faq.answer: Pandorum (2009) is best described as Action, Horror, Mystery, Science Fiction, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Pandorum worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
